Refactorization points  

- Refactor how we initialize bq client and pubsub. Should it use the new factory?  
    
- Avoid converting to tuples 
    

- Can we use insert_rows_from_json or dataframe?  
    
- Append_final_rows doesn’t need to get the table. Just insert rows with reference.  (append_finals_rows.py line 52)  [https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/samples/bigquery-table-insert-rows#bigquery_table_insert_rows-python](https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/samples/bigquery-table-insert-rows#bigquery_table_insert_rows-python) 
    
- When do we get retrieval not succesfull? Should we be writing then?  
    
- What’s variables cell reference Rename sample_content_variable_reference to sample_content_field_name  
    
- What’s the grain?  
    

Restriction on insert rows: The size that you can send out is 10 MB. This leads to us not being able to having unnesting. 

Unnesting creates a row for each value 

Each row contains additional information about the sample for that row 

If we're able to handle the Nan in alp table than we can use pandas dataframes.  

Haven't tried the pandas stream inserts. We could get a NaN when we're querying a table.  

retrieval successful - No connection is there, or when we can't find table. Probably not needed in the future, was used 

for debugging.  

Does get table get only the refernce or the entire data? 

We don’t have budget for external support for external search and monitor might get support  

Improvements  

- Can we use flow control for handling spikes? [https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/flow-control](https://cloud.google.com/pubsub/docs/flow-control). Sebastian tried that and it could be used but it’s a bit more overhead

28/04  

Update from town hall  

Reduce positions Big part of the headquarter is reducing positions. reducing positions 10% and reducing cost by 10 %. Technology is not included. Reduce budget for externals and supply centers. In the townhall they presented figures and for competitions side. Mediamarkt technology doesn’t seem to be impacted to as big extent. Tech is not in scope as of right now. (The new LPO will have a) overview in August and this is when we will start seeing impact probably. Dieter isn’t worried since analytics isn’t, he think if technology it’s old tech, but analytics have grown a lot the past years and it’s an area where MMS has invested a lot in.  IT already reduced 3 times and MMS have problem to hire new problem.  

Sales 

Sales are stable, but if you also account for inflation it’s not that good.The whole economy is stagnat however so not specific not MMS . 

Margin is decreasing from. 400 million to 200 million.  

Dieter interpretation is that they want to reduce complexity.

Notes team time 

Aura system - is responsible to purchase project for suppliers.  

invested in SAP retail to replace there custome system (GMS).  

Integrate data for sub retail.  

Data foundation did modeling  

2 applicatoins running which serves the same function - the old and new system. They have different data models.  

They were involved too late. 2021 and they told us they going live in mars. Moved going live to august and then in mars 2022. 

Lessons learned. Terradata side go live caused incident.  

integrate data into gcp but then needed to back integrate to terradata. Bringing data back to terradata caused a major data.  

1 week no reports was done.  

Planning to do it for germany 2024  

Aura project is about rethinking how we did it in the past.  

Not use BIODS? They’re a consulting firm.  

Mapped new data into the new world. Have no advanatages if you model it to the old way. We need a integrated view.  

Have discussion on data catalog what they expect us to have their 

Mediamarkt have had bad net promoter score before, especially the VPs.

We need to enable ssl to cloud sql if it’s private for best. IF you’re using private network the  sql should be enabled 

Secrets in datahub: 

kubectl create secret generic mysql-secrets --from-literal=mysql-root-password=datahub 

kubectl create secret generic neo4j-secrets --from-literal=neo4j-password=datahub 

Terraform will generate a secret for cloudsql create a secret manager for cloud sql 

During helm chart deployment we need to fetch the secret and put it in the kubernetes cluster secret  

Add secret for datahub user and password for neo4j in terraform

Omni channel reporting and analytics - martin learning collaboration  

Sponsoring intelligent  

Mapped new data to old world 

Take data from terradata and ingest it back again 

Utilize omni channel spine system  

Yesterday kickoff, business commitment. First phase is discovery, what does the business need?  

Per LPO driving business understanding. What is requirement? Central report? Cluster it by silver report, gold report.  

Important since it gives us a lot of visibility.  

Terradata is central gms system and it’s important since it’s consolidate our gms systems. 1000 gms systems and terradata consolidates all that data.  Warp consolidates all the gms data for 1000 thousands servers. Terradata will be consumer but not keep the data it’ll be stored in gcp.  

Gms - purchase products -> delivery -> stock -> sales order -> outbound delivery → billing  

Ocs don’t want to distinguish between offline and online for sales order  

WIll take 6 months to get the requirements but also the  

Kristopher will be PO (more technical)  

Martin said that they’re closing down all the building soon.  

Data access we split into data foundation and services  

Data foundation wanted to do warehouse integration and modeling 

Access teams was responsible for governance and what we’re doing 

Dieter created business object lifecycle  

Two teams split up to data governance  

Data governance with senior people to drive bigger topics

Good feedback datahub 

DC 

Hi data peepz!  

I’ve recently started an assignment where our team is responsible for parts of the clients data governance. Our team is responsible for creating a data catalog using Linkedin’s open source project Datahub to make data more discoverable and easier to access. It will also provide a way of measuring data quality which initiative we’ll drive as well as laying the foundation for handling PII data (smaller part though). I expect that a big part of the project will also be the release and adoption of the data catalog and its features so any knowledge in this area would also be valuable.  

Besides that we also provide consulting services and blueprints for general analytics & engineering challenges (e.g. how to build a simple, event-based data architecture on top of the GCP) but this will probably be a later stage of the project.   

If this sounds like an interesting assignment and you think you have related experience in this, hit me up! ;)  

Tags:  

Python, Java GCP, Kuberneters, Cloud sql, Cloud run, PubSub, Bigquery, Fast API, Datahub, Terraform 

Data Governance, PII, Data Quality, Data Platform 

The next part of the project will be to give more insight to the data - adding  

which tables are new 

how is the quality of the data 

do the tables contain PII data, 

 Also entails the actual release of the data catalog and the adoption of it so any insight in this process will be valuable I believe.

[Labels vs Tags](https://www.doit-intl.com/google-cloud-platform-resource-labeling-best-practices/) 

One distinction that first needs to be made is the difference between labels and tags inside of GCP. 

Up until recently, they had a pseudo-1-to-1 relationship, but now they are separate entities as GCP evolves. The difference now is that tags are a way to group resources together to have a network policy applied to them versus a label that groups resources together for billing and searching purposes (and possibly more in the future).

Jira task 

Expose Rest API or the Kafka server 

Responsibilities 

Extend the existing infrastructure as a code methodology and code libraries 

for MMS Analytics Platform on GCP 

On-Board new product teams to TFE and manage the infrastructure setup based on the customer maturity level and their specific needs. 

Automate build processes and create best practices for analytical applications 

and services. 

Cloud Build, BigQuery, Pub/Sub, TFE, Data catalog (Datahub), PII data, Data Quality, Data Security, Cost and infrastructure optimization 

The team is newly formed since 3 weeks with a new focus. Right now we have the following challenges: 

lower data management costs; and increased access to needed data, Data security 

We now have to centrally control that the lake does not become a swamp. Of course, we have to control this via more data governance, but our colleagues need information from our team. From an engineering perspective, this is about acquiring metadata such as 

- which tables exist 
    
- how often are the tables used 
    
- who uses these tables 
    
- do the tables contain PII data 
    

- are there schema changes 
    
- which tables are new 
    
- how is the quality of the data 
    

We want to build a user interface for our customers (data owners, analysts, engineers, business etc.) to enrich the collected data with business meta data and make it searchable (data cataloging). 

In addition, we provide consulting services and blueprints for general analytics & engineering challenges (e.g. how to build a simple, event-based data architecture on top of the GCP). 

Hello Channel! For my current assignment we have 100k+ tables that we would like to ingest into Datahub. For all the tables we want to retrieve the information contained in labels and add it as metadata in Datahub. What’s a feasible way of achieving this?  

I was looking into the default export of metadata but it doesn’t seem to export the data contained in the labels?
